questions.question: Why was Sven Olsen considered one of the best construction workers on the station?

options.B: He could precisely position and fuse floating girders in zero gravity

questions.explanation: Sven excelled at collecting floating girders and positioning them correctly in zero gravity before fusing them together.

questions.question: Why was Sven able to qualify easily for the 150-pound bonus?

options.B: He had a naturally wiry and lightweight body

questions.explanation: Sven had a thin and wiry body, which helped him qualify for the weight limit required for space workers.

questions.question: What advantage did construction workers in space have over workers on Earth?

options.B: They had better machines
options.C: They could move freely without gravity pulling them down

questions.explanation: In space there is no gravity pulling them away from their work, so they could float and examine their work easily.

questions.question: Why did Sven believe Claribel was a sensible choice as a pet in space?

options.B: She was light, needed little food, and adapted well to zero gravity
options.D: She was trained for space travel

questions.explanation: Claribel was lightweight, required little food, and was not bothered by the absence of gravity.

questions.question: Why did the narrator initially think the whistle sound came from the intercom?

options.A: The intercom often made similar sounds
options.B: He expected a station announcement
options.C: He could not see Claribel immediately

questions.explanation: The narrator assumed the whistle came from the station intercom and waited for an announcement.

questions.question: What shows that Claribel had already adapted well to zero gravity?

options.A: She slept most of the time
options.B: She performed loops and floated effortlessly

questions.explanation: Claribel performed a backward loop and floated easily in the absence of gravity.

questions.question: Why did Sven delay confessing that Claribel belonged to him?

options.B: He feared punishment for bringing a pet aboard
options.C: He wanted to surprise the crew
options.D: He did not like the bird

questions.explanation: Pets were unofficially forbidden on the station, so Sven initially kept it secret.

questions.question: Why was it sometimes difficult to hide Claribel from visiting officials?

options.B: She made loud whistles and peeps

questions.explanation: Claribel sometimes made loud sounds which could reveal her presence.

questions.question: What unusual condition affected the station before the air purifier malfunctioned?

options.B: A rare eclipse by Earth’s shadow

questions.explanation: A rare eclipse caused part of the air purifier to freeze.

questions.question: Why did the narrator initially feel confused and sluggish when he woke up?

options.B: The air quality had already begun deteriorating

questions.explanation: The low oxygen level likely caused the narrator’s headache and sluggish thinking.

questions.question: Why did the crew pass the oxygen bottle around 'like a pipe of peace'?

options.B: They all needed oxygen and shared it calmly

questions.explanation: They shared oxygen while discussing the situation calmly.

questions.question: Why did Jim initially reject the narrator’s warning about the air?

options.A: He believed the alarm system would detect the problem
options.B: He did not trust the narrator

questions.explanation: Jim believed the alarm systems would automatically detect any air problems.

questions.question: What caused Jim to realize that the situation was serious?

options.B: His assistant reminded him that the second alarm circuit was not connected

questions.explanation: Jim realized the backup alarm system had not been installed yet.

questions.question: Why does the narrator say that without Claribel they would have been 'slightly dead'?

options.B: Because the air problem would have gone unnoticed
options.C: Because they would have left the station

questions.explanation: Claribel’s collapse warned them before the air became completely fatal.

questions.question: What literary effect does the author create by calling Claribel a 'general pet'?

options.A: Humor and warmth among the crew
options.B: Fear

questions.explanation: The phrase shows how the crew affectionately shared the bird as their pet.

questions.question: What central idea does Claribel’s role in the story represent?

options.B: Nature can sometimes detect danger better than machines

questions.explanation: Claribel detects the danger when the technology fails.

questions.question: Why did the crew not initially suspect a problem with the air system?

options.A: They trusted the automatic alarm system
options.B: They ignored the warning signs

questions.explanation: The crew relied on the automated alarm systems to detect any problem.

questions.question: Which theme is best reflected through Claribel’s presence in the story?

options.B: Power of technology

questions.explanation: Claribel becomes both a companion and a lifesaver for the crew.

questions.question: What narrative technique does the author use when the narrator slowly realizes the cause of the danger?

options.B: Gradual realization

questions.explanation: The narrator slowly remembers miners using canaries to detect gases.

questions.question: Why does the author end the story by mentioning birds in every space station?

options.A: To emphasize their usefulness as safety measures
options.B: To make the story funny

questions.explanation: The ending highlights the importance of birds as an additional safety system.

questions.question: Which event acts as the turning point in the story?

options.B: Claribel collapsing

questions.explanation: Claribel collapsing leads the crew to discover the air problem.

questions.question: What does the story mainly suggest about human dependence on machines?

options.B: Machines should not be used
options.C: Humans should not rely completely on machines

questions.explanation: The failure of the alarm system shows that human observation is also necessary.

questions.question: Which quality of the narrator helped the crew discover the problem?

options.B: Observation and reasoning

questions.explanation: The narrator connected Claribel’s collapse with miners’ use of canaries.

questions.question: What does the story ultimately emphasize about small events?

options.B: They may reveal serious problems

questions.explanation: Claribel’s small action revealed a life-threatening danger.

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes the central message of the story?

options.B: Observation and nature can help humans detect danger when technology fails
options.C: Animals should not be kept as pets

questions.explanation: The story highlights observation, curiosity, and nature’s role in detecting danger.
